title: 58% of PRs in our largest monorepo merge without human review
cleaned_text:
# 58% of PRs in our largest monorepo merge without human review

Source newsletter: Anthropic's revenue spike 📈, Sam Altman excludes CFO💼, how Meta builds context 🤖
Sender: TLDR <dan@tldrnewsletter.com>
Published At: 2026-04-07T10:59:25+00:00
Canonical URL: https://vercel.com/blog/58-percent-of-prs-in-our-largest-monorepo-merge-without-human-review

## Newsletter Context

Section: Programming, Design & Data Science

One of Vercel's oldest and largest Next.js apps is a monorepo that contains multiple critical properties. The repository sees over 400 pull requests per week on average. Until recently, this required human approval before merging, but now, an agent reviews and merges 58% of those pull requests without a human reviewer. This has dropped the average merge time by 62%.

reason_trace.judge_reason: The document is about software development automation and agents, which has some indirect relevance to the user's interest in LLM tooling and reasoning, but it does not directly address core LLM architecture or evaluation.

This has dropped the average merge time by 62%.
